codeReview.summary: The change is narrowly scoped to client/tests/test_tui.py and directly addresses the worker-thread subprocess leak by patching cc_sentiment.signing.discovery.subprocess.run in test_setup_check_remotes_cancel_on_remote_back. The updated assertions verify the remote worker cancellation and confirm only gh auth status subprocess calls are intercepted during the exercised path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission/shared docs do not currently note that the remote-back path can execute KeyDiscovery gh auth checks from a Textual worker thread, where stubbing at higher layers may miss the subprocess boundary. Capturing this test pattern would help prevent similar flakes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: client/tests/test_tui.py:2850 patches cc_sentiment.signing.discovery.subprocess.run for this path; handoff verification documents pre-fix TimeoutExpired(['gh', 'auth', 'status'], 10) and post-fix stability across 20 runs.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill, and commit 3bfad19. The feature meets its stated intent with a focused test-only fix, no scope creep, and no blocking code issues found.
